title: Fix Navbar Visibility – Always Show Home, Dashboard, Admin Panel, and Login/Logout

requirements.text: Rewrite frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx so that all navigation elements are always rendered and never hidden due to layout or overflow issues. The navbar must use a flex layout with `flex-nowrap` and `overflow-visible` on the desktop nav container so items are never clipped or collapsed. The three nav links must be: 'Home' (always visible, navigates to /), 'Dashboard' (visible only when the user is authenticated, navigates to /dashboard), and 'Admin Panel' (visible only when the user is authenticated AND isAdmin is true, navigates to /admin). The Login/Logout button must always be visible on the right side of the navbar, never wrapped or hidden. On desktop (md and above), all visible links and the Login/Logout button must appear in a single horizontal row without overflow. On mobile, the hamburger menu must include the same conditional link set (Home always, Dashboard if authenticated, Admin Panel if authenticated + isAdmin) plus the Login/Logout action. The mobile menu must open and close reliably with no z-index or overflow conflicts.

quotes: in navbar clearly show home,dashboard,and admin for admin and login logout button because some times navbar hide button please fix this problem

acceptanceCriteria: On desktop, 'Home' link is always visible in the navbar.
On desktop, 'Dashboard' link is visible when the user is authenticated and hidden when not authenticated.
On desktop, 'Admin Panel' link is visible when the user is authenticated AND isCallerAdmin returns true; it is hidden for non-admin users.
On desktop, the Login/Logout button is always visible and never clipped or hidden by overflow.
No navbar items are ever hidden due to CSS overflow, flex-wrap, or z-index issues on any screen size.
On mobile, the hamburger icon is always visible and tapping it reliably toggles the mobile menu open/closed.
The mobile menu shows the same conditional link set (Home always, Dashboard if authenticated, Admin Panel if authenticated + isAdmin) plus a Login/Logout button.
The mobile menu has no z-index or overflow conflicts that would cause it to be hidden behind other elements.
The navbar layout does not break or clip items on viewport widths between 320px and 1440px.

requirements.text: In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, ensure the useIsCallerAdmin query: (1) is enabled only when identity is non-null, (2) uses a fresh authenticated actor created directly with the identity (not a cached anonymous actor) to call the backend isCallerAdmin() function, (3) uses ['isCallerAdmin', principalString] as the query key so it re-fetches whenever the logged-in principal changes, and (4) has staleTime: 0 and gcTime: 0 so it never returns a stale cached result. This ensures the Admin Panel link appears reliably after login without a page refresh.

quotes: some times navbar hide button please fix this problem

acceptanceCriteria: useIsCallerAdmin is not enabled when identity is null/undefined.
useIsCallerAdmin uses an authenticated actor (created with the current identity) to call isCallerAdmin().
The query key includes the principal string so it re-fetches on identity change.
staleTime and gcTime are both set to 0.
After login, the admin check re-fetches automatically without requiring a page refresh.
Non-admin users receive false from the query.

requirements.text: In frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, add or verify a useEffect that watches the identity object and, whenever the identity changes from null/undefined to a non-null value (user has just logged in), calls queryClient.invalidateQueries() to invalidate all queries (including isCallerAdmin) so they immediately re-fetch with the new authenticated identity. This prevents the navbar from showing stale unauthenticated state after login.

quotes: some times navbar hide button please fix this problem

acceptanceCriteria: After Internet Identity login completes, all React Query queries (including isCallerAdmin) are invalidated and re-fetched automatically.
The Admin Panel link appears in the navbar for the admin user immediately after login without a page refresh.
The Dashboard link appears for authenticated non-admin users immediately after login without a page refresh.
The Login button changes to a Logout button immediately after login.

constraints: Do not modify any file under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts immutable sections, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not change backend logic or data models.
Do not change Test Page timer, navigation, or submission logic.
The three navbar links must remain: Home (always), Dashboard (authenticated), Admin Panel (authenticated + admin only).
nonGoals: Adding new navigation links beyond Home, Dashboard, and Admin Panel.
Changing the visual design theme or color palette.
Changing the Test Page, Dashboard page, or Admin Panel page content.
